commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f27129b4d7b5e812831157ed404463b6cf630c8e)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a governed managed TaskFlow finalizer in `src/tasks/task-registry.ts` plus regression tests for parent closeout delivery, manual-policy opt-out, active-child waits, and delivery-runtime fallback.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding by source inspection: a governed Discord flow with `ownerKey=agent:main:discord:channel:123:thread:456` and `requesterOrigin.threadId` reaches the new branch and builds `to=channel:123:thread:456`. I did not run the PR tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor or maintainer changes plus exact source-branch proof; automation should not take over while proof is insufficient.

target.title: fix(taskflow): finalize governed managed flows
target.body:
## Summary

- add a narrow OpenClaw-native finalizer for managed governed TaskFlows (controllerId=governed-taskflow/v1)
- when all linked child tasks are terminal, close the governed flow, persist terminal summary state, and queue/deliver exactly one parent closeout
- suppress duplicate per-child terminal delivery once the governed flow owns the parent closeout

## Why this is narrower than #61242

I reviewed #61242 first. It has the right direction, but it is stale and broad: it changes sessions_spawn, retry, CLI, UI, docs, and managed flow UX across 52 files. Existing review already flagged contract/security blockers, especially current-main sessions_spawn schema drift and ACP inherited-tool policy risk.

This PR intentionally does not rebuild that full surface. It extracts the proven invariant from field failures and the local hotfix: managed governed flows must terminalize and produce one parent-visible closeout when their linked children are all terminal.

## Behavior

For managed flows with controllerId="governed-taskflow/v1":

- active child tasks prevent terminalization
- terminal child tasks are summarized into stateJson.terminalSummary
- stateJson.parentSummaryDelivery records queued/delivered/failed status with a deterministic context key
- direct requester delivery is attempted when a deliverable requester origin exists
- fallback queues a parent session event and wakes heartbeat
- child task terminal delivery is marked not_applicable so the parent does not get both a child task message and a governed closeout
- stateJson.autoCloseOnAllChildrenTerminal=false or terminalCloseoutPolicy="manual" opt out of auto-close

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: managed governed TaskFlows could stay running after linked child tasks had already reached terminal states, so final parent-channel governed closeout depended on manual pickup or local bridge repair. This PR ports the proven governed-finalizer invariant into source so the flow terminalizes and emits one parent closeout from core task registry state.

Real environment tested: local native OpenClaw install on macOS, gateway LaunchAgent, Discord governed execution parent channel, with the same governed-finalizer invariant hot-patched into the installed runtime bundle before this source-port PR.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- Hot-patched the local installed OpenClaw runtime with the governed finalizer invariant that this PR ports into TypeScript source.
- Restarted the gateway.
- Ran governed worker and validator canary flows through the live Discord parent-channel path.
- Verified managed TaskFlow terminal state and parentSummaryDelivery state from runtime TaskFlow records.
- Verified the parent closeout was posted visibly in Discord without a manual follow-up prompt.

Evidence after fix:

```text
Manual worker -> validator canary flow ddc6f1d8-b972-4eda-9d5e-334c67336ea reached:
status=succeeded
parentSummaryDelivery.status=delivered

Automatic compact canary flow 1fa9233a-337b-4b3a-abf9-3ffcfa4bbe98 reached:
status=succeeded
parentSummaryDelivery.status=delivered
posted one compact parent closeout message in Discord
```

Observed result after fix: when linked child tasks were terminal, the managed governed flow moved to terminal state instead of staying running, persisted stateJson.parentSummaryDelivery, and delivered exactly one compact parent-channel closeout. The source change in this PR preserves that invariant without relying on update-fragile dist edits.

What was not tested: a freshly built OpenClaw package from this exact PR branch installed into the live gateway; non-Discord delivery channels; Docker installs.

## Validation

```bash
corepack pnpm exec tsgo --noEmit --pretty false --project tsconfig.json
corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Results:

- typecheck passed
- src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts: 72 passed
- diff whitespace check passed

## Scope Notes

- no sessions_spawn schema changes
- no ACP inherited-tool policy changes
- no retry/UI/docs expansion
- no local plugin/dist patch dependency

Related #61242.
target.author: dolencluka

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: DolencLuka
commands.command:
re-review

review blockers have been addressed in f27129b4d7b5:
- child-terminal suppression now only applies after a governed parent summary delivery state exists, so manual/auto-close opt-out flows keep normal child delivery
- delivery-runtime load failures now mark parent summary delivery failed and enqueue the parent-session fallback
- added focused regressions for both manual/auto-close opt-out child delivery and runtime-load fallback

local validation:
- corepack pnpm exec tsgo --noemit --pretty false --project tsconfig.json
- corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts (75 passed)
- git diff --check

current ci note: all relevant pr/security/proof checks passed except checks-node-core-fast, which failed in unrelated src/infra/outbound/source-delivery-plan.test.ts by 120s timeout; i do not have repo admin rights to rerun the failed job.
commands.freeform_prompt:
re-review

Review blockers have been addressed in f27129b4d7b5:
- child-terminal suppression now only applies after a governed parent summary delivery state exists, so manual/auto-close opt-out flows keep normal child delivery
- delivery-runtime load failures now mark parent summary delivery failed and enqueue the parent-session fallback
- added focused regressions for both manual/auto-close opt-out child delivery and runtime-load fallback

Local validation:
- corepack pnpm exec tsgo --noEmit --pretty false --project tsconfig.json
- corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts (75 passed)
- git diff --check

Current CI note: all relevant PR/security/proof checks passed except checks-node-core-fast, which failed in unrelated src/infra/outbound/source-delivery-plan.test.ts by 120s timeout; I do not have repo admin rights to rerun the failed job.
